title: grewia biloba
intro: Grewia biloba, also known as the Bilwa or Wood Apple, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Grewia biloba include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Grewia biloba include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
